---
title: "Roberto Macrì Demartino"
pagetitle: "Roberto Macrì Demartino"
subtitle: "Postdoctoral Researcher in Statistics at the University of Trieste"
image: ./images/Roberto_Profile.jpg
about:
  template: solana
  image-width: 15em
  links:
    - text: Email
      icon: envelope
      href: "mailto:roberto.macridemartino@deams.units.it"
    - text: Linkedin
      icon: linkedin
      aria-label: "LinkedIn"
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/roberto-macr%C3%AD-demartino-a51459204/
    - text: Github
      icon: github
      aria-label: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/RoMaD-96
    # - icon: mastodon
    #   href: https://hachyderm.io/@spcanelon
    - text: "{{< ai orcid >}} ORCID"
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-5296-6566
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
      aria-label: "Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=eAGkT8kAAAAJ&hl=it

page-layout: full

execute: 
  freeze: false
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

::: {style="text-align: left;"}
# About me

Hi! I am Roberto, a Postdoctoral Researcher in Bayesian Statistics at the
[Department of Economics, Business, Mathematics and Statistics “Bruno de Finetti”](https://deams.units.it/en/department/people/macr%C3%AD-demartino-roberto/317936)
of the [University of Trieste](https://portale.units.it/en). My research focuses on the
development and application of Bayesian statistical methods, with particular interest in
borrowing historical information, replication studies, variable selection, survival analysis, and sports analytics.

I obtained both my B.Sc. and M.Sc. in Statistics from the
[University of Calabria](https://www.unical.it/?lang=en). I then earned a Ph.D. in Statistical
Science from the [University of Padova](https://www.unipd.it/en/), under the supervision of
[Prof. Nicola Torelli](https://scholar.google.it/citations?user=l5fIs0wAAAAJ&hl=it) and
[Prof. Leonardo Egidi](https://scholar.google.it/citations?hl=it&user=Ux1LY_Qx7T8C). My doctoral
dissertation focused on Bayesian methods for borrowing historical information.

During my Ph.D., I was a visiting research scholar at the
[Athens University of Economics and Business](https://www.aueb.gr/en) under the supervision of
[Prof. Ioannis Ntzoufras](https://scholar.google.it/citations?user=nZahG6wAAAAJ&hl=it), and at the
[University of Zurich](https://www.uzh.ch/en.html) under the supervision of
[Prof. Leonhard Held](https://scholar.google.it/citations?hl=it&user=KKqXo7sAAAAJ). More recently,
I was a visiting researcher at [Aalto University](https://www.aalto.fi/en), working with the
[Bayesian Workflow Group](https://users.aalto.fi/~ave/group.html) under the supervision of
[Prof. Aki Vehtari](https://users.aalto.fi/~ave/index.html).
:::

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8 style="text-align: left;"}
## Education

- **_Ph.D._ in Statistical Science**, **2025**  
  *University of Padova, Italy*

- **_M.Sc._ in Statistics and Computer Science**, **2021**  
  *University of Calabria, Italy*

- **_B.Sc._ in Statistics**, **2018**  
  *University of Calabria, Italy*
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 style="text-align: left;"}
## Interests

- Bayesian methods
- Prior elicitation
- Replication studies
- Survival analysis
- Variable selection
- Models for sports data
- Historical information borrowing 
- Mixture models
:::

::::
